channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Double Fine Workers File to Unionize
item.content:encoded:
Double Fine Productions, known for the Psychonauts series and more recently Kiln, has filed a petition to unionize. The studio is currently owned by Microsoft, but on May 7, employees filed a petition with the National Labor Relations Board, making their intent to unionize clear. According to the filing, 42 employees work at the Microsoft-owned studio. That number includes all regular part-time and full-time employees.

The Union is being formed in conjunction with the Communication Workers of America. In 2025, the CWA helped establish an industry-wide union, United Video Game Workers. That particular union, which is a direct-join union, is for workers in the industry who have been laid off or aren’t employed at any specific studio. Now, Double Fine has become the latest Microsoft-owned studio to see its workers unionize.

Double Fine Becomes Latest Microsoft Studio to Unionize

In a statement released to Aftermath, which exclusively broke the news, the CWA touted the move as an effort to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to, among other things, diversity and inclusion. They also thanked Microsoft for taking a neutral approach to the news and not interfering with workers’ rights to organize.

“On May 7, the workers at Microsoft studio Double Fine Productions announced their decision to form a union with CWA to preserve and extend the studio’s commitments to creative excellence, diversity and inclusion, and worker quality of life,” the statement reads. “In tandem with requesting voluntary recognition from the company, workers have also filed an election petition with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to secure union representation. We appreciate that Microsoft has taken a neutral approach and agreed not to interfere in any way with workers’ rights to organize unions.”

The CWA is no stranger to Microsoft. In July of last year, they stated they were disappointed in the tech conglomerate’s decision to lay off thousands of workers at a time when the company was prospering. Read that full statement from July 2025 here.

Double Fine released Kiln, an online party brawler built around pottery, on April 23, 2026. Check out the full content roadmap for the game here.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: CDL Major 3 Tournament Guide—Schedule, New Format, and Teams
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League’s Major 3 tournament draws closer, and it’s set to shake up the competitive season. As one of the most ambitious and sizeable events in the calendar, Major 3 introduces a new format, a $365,000 prize pool, and a spot for COD fans in North America to enjoy the action live at DreamHack Atlanta.

What makes Major 3 more important is that it’s a Pro-Am event, set to feature the talents of four Challengers teams, all well worth a shot at the title. Below, you’ll find out everything you need to know about the Call of Duty League’s Major 3 tournament.

CDL Major 3 2026 Schedule and Format

Major 3 is set to run from May 15th to May 17th. It’s a short-form event, but there’s plenty of meat on the bones, including a Pro-Am element and a group stage, which is very rare in the Call of Duty space.

Here’s how it all works:

Group Stage

  • 16 teams competing (12 COD League and four Challengers teams)
  • Four GSL groups represented by four teams apiece
  • Best-of-five format
  • The top two teams in each group enter the Playoffs

Playoffs

  • Traditional double-elimination bracket
  • Best-of-five series format
  • Best-of-seven series for the Grand Final

The prize distribution is as follows:

  • 1st Place: $150,000
  • 2nd Place: $90,000
  • 3rd Place: $50,000
  • 4th Place: $30,000
  • 5th – 6th Place: $15,000
  • 7th – 8th Place: $7,500
  • 9th – 16th Place: $0

Here’s a better look at the CDL Major 3 schedule:

Friday, May 15

  • Group Stage Matches

Saturday, May 16

  • Winner’s & Loser’s Bracket (inc. Finals)

Sunday, May 17

  • Grand Final only

COD League Major 3 Teams (Including Challengers)

As I’ve explained, sixteen teams are competing at Major 3, but for some, it’ll be a one-series-and-done kind of weekend.

Here’s the spread of teams going into Major 3, with the CDL organizations ranked as they were in the standings at the time of writing:

  • OpTic Texas
  • LA Thieves
  • Toronto KOI
  • FaZe Vegas
  • Paris Gentle Mates
  • Riyadh Falcons
  • Vancouver Surge
  • G2 Minnesota
  • Carolina Royal Ravens
  • Miami Heretics
  • Cloud9 New York
  • Boston Breach
  • Huntsmen
  • OMiT Noir
  • P7 Notorious
  • ROC Esports

How to Watch CDL Major 3 at DreamHack

If you’re not at DreamHack Atlanta, you can watch the third Major of the season via the usual channels:

  • COD League on YouTube
  • COD League on Twitch

CDL Major 3 2026 Predictions

OpTic Texas approaches the third Major of the 2026 season in white-hot form, and the squad is looking for a slick redemption story after failing at the last post against FaZe Vegas at DreamHack Birmingham.

Behind OpTic Texas, LA Thieves is the next big team in the predictions stakes, with the ‘Super Team‘ hunting a trophy, the likes of which has avoided the organization for almost a year, not counting the recent CDL Minor win.

Of course, those participating in some Call of Duty betting will want to watch out for the odds tied to those Challengers teams. Both ROC and P7 put up a phenomenal performance at DreamHack Birmingham, and P7 in particular has something to prove.

The P7 squad is represented entirely by former COD League talent, including some of the most recent removals from the top tier, Beans and Snoopy.

To play it safe, back an obvious team like OpTic, LAT, or even FaZe Vegas. If you want wild odds, hit up an outsider.

Are you going to be tuning into the CDL Major 3 action when it starts? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the World Series of Warzone is back

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1063)—Answers for Saturday, May 9
item.content:encoded:
It’s a quiet Saturday, but before you get started with your weekend, you need to unlock your puzzling potential and complete today’s NYT Connections challenge. If you’re not feeling up to the task, don’t worry. I’m here with the NYT Connections hints and tips you need to complete the puzzle as quickly as possible.

Read on to get today’s NYT Connections answers and explanations!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 9)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • PUFF
  • EPISODE
  • TIE
  • SHOELACES
  • PERIOD
  • CHAIN
  • SEASON
  • THINK
  • LANYARD
  • FRIENDSHIP BRACELET
  • CONVERSATION
  • SERIES
  • QUIPU
  • BOA
  • FRANCHISE
  • MACRAME

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Ways you’d measure a television show
  • Green – Things you wear around your neck
  • Blue – Things you can tie knots in
  • Purple – Things you’d follow with the word ‘piece’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 9:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 9 (#1063)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – EPISODE, FRANCHISE, SEASON, SERIES
  • Green – BOA, CHAIN, LANYARD, TIE
  • Blue – FRIENDSHIP BRACELET, MACRAME, QUIPU, SHOELACES
  • Purple – CONVERSATION, PERIOD, PUFF, THINK

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
